title: Headache with visual symptoms

red_flags.text: Supports sudden onset, neurological deficit, fever, trauma, pattern change, unexplained vomiting, further investigation or referral, and structured headache-diary fields.
red_flags.rationale: Supports headache red flags and diary fields for headache with visual symptoms; no decision is generated.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports sudden onset, neurological deficit, fever, trauma, pattern change, unexplained vomiting, further investigation or referral, and structured headache-diary fields.

red_flags.text: Lists sudden visual loss, sudden vision change, flashes or floaters with retinal signs, retinal detachment, acute diplopia, severe eye pain with nausea or vomiting, pain on eye movement, and new pupil defects.
red_flags.rationale: Supports sudden visual change, acute diplopia, severe pain and pupil-defect urgency context for headache with visual symptoms; no decision is generated.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists sudden visual loss, sudden vision change, flashes or floaters with retinal signs, retinal detachment, acute diplopia, severe eye pain with nausea or vomiting, pain on eye movement, and new pupil defects.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Headache onset and features, visual symptom and eye, duration, field, diplopia, pain, neurological symptoms, acuity, examination and diagnosis are absent.
No diagnosis, interpretation, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for headache with visual symptoms.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
